acceptance_checklist.update_policy: Mark an item only after every criterion and verifier reference has durable evidence.

items.description: Record the actual definition, database, image, Runner, API, Core, and browser baseline without support claims.
acceptance_criteria: all 13 formal slugs have a documented evidence state
no Worker receives an inferred support claim

items.description: Make the embedded definition the checked Worker creation input and retain every product-facing field.
acceptance_criteria: Definition bundle hashes are checked at creation time
database projections cannot silently diverge

items.description: Reject unknown adapters, remove mock substitutions, and remove frontend credential form fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: unknown adapters fail explicitly
mock binaries and unsupported form mappings are rejected

items.description: Create an opt-in disposable fixture for authenticated product-path verification.
acceptance_criteria: fixture identities are disposable and isolated
authenticated browser validation has deterministic setup and cleanup

items.description: Prove the Codex CLI special-adapter product path with real image, Runner, API, and browser evidence.
acceptance_criteria: real image, Runner, API, Core, and browser evidence are recorded
no formal deployment claim is made from local proof

items.description: Prove the Gemini CLI generic ACP product path without command-derived adapter selection.
acceptance_criteria: generic ACP behavior is explicit
missing authorized model resources block visible progress without fallback

items.description: Audit selected Worker credential, launch, and adapter contracts against real commands, then remove any unsupported product mapping without promotion.
acceptance_criteria: real command evidence drives credential and launch mappings
unsupported paths remain blocked rather than inferred

items.description: Run every remaining Worker through the same evidence model and record verified or blocked outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: every formal Worker has exactly one run
every run is verified_local_dev or reproducibly blocked

items.description: Independently review the matrix and run the terminal verifier without publishing or deploying.
acceptance_criteria: terminal verifier is run without weakening
outstanding human gates are recorded

responsibilities: execute one bounded implementation or research task

ambiguity_triggers: upstream license or redistribution status is unclear
protocol behavior has no official evidence
a public API or migration change has no approved contract

assumption_policy.allowed: Only low-risk evidence ordering and organization may be assumed.

block_if: a real secret value is needed
a product security boundary is unclear

secondary_user_query.prompt: Which license, protocol, credential, or public-contract decision should govern this Worker?
required_when: a Worker cannot reach a machine-checkable verdict

allowed_when: Worker runs have no overlapping write paths
research and review need independent context
one Worker task exceeds the primary context budget
disallowed_when: two agents would write the same Definition, API, migration, or verifier
subagent_activation.token_budget_policy: Subagents return only decisions, changed paths, commands, exit codes, and evidence references under 2000 tokens.

compaction.summary_contract: Preserve task state, accepted evidence, blockers, verifier results, budget exits, and human gates.

context_policy.tool_output_trimming: Keep commands, exit codes, failing assertions, changed paths, and evidence refs; exclude raw logs and secrets.

cost_policy.optimization_goal: Maximize independently verifiable evidence while preventing unbounded Worker retries.

escalation_required_when: a security or public API decision is needed

allowed_decisions: choose the next eligible evidence task

drift_checks: state loop id matches the canonical manifest
active task maps to a declared atomic task
shared-contract changes do not bypass Worker evidence
no human gate is silently crossed

user_confirmation.prompt: May the decision proxy make only the listed low-risk evidence sequencing and public-metadata retry decisions?

split_until: one Worker slug has one isolated evidence run
decomposition_policy.minimum_task_granularity: one definition, runtime, Runner, product, browser, or review evidence slice
decomposition_policy.dependency_policy: A Worker cannot be promoted unless all applicable Definition, runtime, Runner, product, browser, and review gates pass.

escalation.condition: A human gate, blocker, budget exit, or no-progress exit is reached.

failure_modes.mitigation: Verify manifest, tasks, monitoring, and state identifiers together before every running cycle.
Require real runtime, Runner, product, browser, and review evidence before any support claim.
Protect verifier paths and require independent review evidence.

loop_nodes.purpose: Select one bounded evidence task, preserve failures, and stop at a deterministic terminal state.
entry_conditions: current budget has not been exhausted
steps: run the next eligible deterministic verifier

children.purpose: Advance one Worker only with real runtime, Runner, product, browser, and review evidence.
entry_conditions: the Worker has a formal Definition
the selected task has no unresolved human gate
exit_conditions: the Worker has accepted terminal evidence
the Worker has reproducible blocked evidence
the Worker retry budget is exhausted
steps: make one scoped change or record one blocker

metadata.name: Worker Integration Evidence Rebuild
metadata.description: Rebuild every formal Do Worker integration from Definition through real runtime and browser evidence.

objective.user_goal: Verify every formal Worker type with real Definition, image, Runner, credential, product, and browser evidence.
objective.business_value: Replace scattered Worker integration claims with a complete, evidence-backed verification workflow.
objective.done_definition: Every formal Worker has accepted evidence or a reproducible blocked decision, the catalog terminal verifier exits zero, and independent review records human gates.
non_goals: Do not push, merge, or deploy automatically.
Do not use real credentials without human approval.
Do not promote local proof to formal deployment support.

portability_policy.platform_selection_query: Which execution adapter should run this Worker evidence rebuild?

forbidden_behaviors: using mock binaries as real Worker artifacts

failure: required upstream artifact cannot be obtained
a protected verifier would need weakening
a required public contract lacks approval

anti_gaming_rules: do not reduce the formal Worker catalog to hide gaps
